implemented.details: Stage management on /schedule. Server component loads the user's most recent active project, its stages (ordered by order_index), and per-stage task counts via a single grouped select. StageManager client component handles the full lifecycle. AC-SM-1: "+ Add Stage" button opens a modal with a text name field (max 80, validated client-side via validateStageInput — pure fn shared with the server action) and a 6-swatch color picker (earth brown default + frame green / ochre / ink / slate blue / brick red). Submit calls createStage server action which revalidates ownership, fetches max(order_index) for the project, and inserts with order_index=max+1 so the new stage appears at the end. AC-SM-2: drag any stage row (draggable=true) onto another → handleDrop calls reorderStages() pure helper which sorts by order_index, splices the moved stage into the target position, and renumbers all stages to a dense 0..N-1 sequence. The new ordering is committed via reorderProjectStages server action which uses a two-phase update (tombstone with negative indices then write final slots) to stay safe under any future UNIQUE(project_id, order_index) constraint. Up/down arrow buttons provide the same reorder path for touch/accessibility users. AC-SM-3: delete on a stage with tasks opens a confirm dialog using buildDeleteWarning(name, taskCount) which produces the exact spec copy ("N task(s) — deleting removes them all") with correct 1/N pluralization. Only the "Delete stage and tasks" button in the dialog calls deleteStage(..., confirmed=true). AC-SM-4: delete on an empty stage (task_count=0) skips the dialog and calls deleteStage(..., confirmed=false) which the server treats as an unconditional delete because it independently counts tasks and finds zero. deleteStage also re-densifies order_index on remaining stages so gaps never accumulate. 20 new pure unit tests covering validation (empty/whitespace/over-length names, invalid+valid 3/6-digit hex colors), reorderStages (head→tail, tail→head, middle forward/backward, noop, out-of-range clamping, unknown id, non-mutation, unsorted input), normalizeStageColor fallback, and buildDeleteWarning (0/1/N pluralization). Total suite: 85 tests passing. Typecheck + production build + lint all clean.

Customization screen rendered at /setup for projects where a template has already been applied (status='setup' AND stages exist). Server component loads stages/tasks/task_dependencies (filtered to in-project ids only) and passes to CustomizationScreen client component. buildCustomizationTree (pure) groups tasks under stages by order_index — AC-TC-1 collapsible tree with per-stage header + checkbox per task. tasksWithBrokenDependencies (pure) returns the set of tasks whose direct depends_on_task_id is currently toggled off; the tree renders a ⚠ icon with title tooltip on each — AC-TC-2. Note: template-apply does not yet write task_dependencies rows (templates carry no dep info per prior factory_decision), so the warning is wired + tested but dormant until the task-management cycle adds dep editing. AI assist panel (AC-TC-3): chat textarea → suggestFromMessage keyword match (ICF → toggle_off slab + add ICF wall; insulation → add upgrade task; solar → add PV task) returns AssistantSuggestion with title/rationale/changes; each suggestion renders Accept/Reject buttons. Accept (AC-TC-4) calls applySuggestion merging toggle_off and add_task changes into the client draft state and marks the chat entry applied; Reject marks dismissed. applyCustomization server action persists the draft: deletes removed task ids (cascade removes materials + dep rows), inserts added tasks with max(order_index)+1 per stage and conservative planned_start=project.start_date. finalizeProject server action (AC-TC-5) verifies ownership + at-least-one-stage + at-least-one-task, updates projects.status setup→active, revalidates layout, redirects to /briefing. 22 new unit tests across tree-building ordering, toggle diff, broken-dep detection (including not flagging the off task itself and transitive vs direct), suggestion ICF/insulation/solar/empty-string cases + stable-id determinism, and applySuggestion merging across existing draft state. Total suite: 65 tests passing. Typecheck + production build clean (19 routes, Proxy middleware detected).

Template picker UI on /setup loads the 3 seeded residential templates (Standard Timber Frame, Slab-on-Grade Single Storey, ICF Two-Storey) and summarizes each server-side via summarizeTemplate (AC-TB-1). Click-to-select highlights the active card and drives the preview panel showing stages with colored left-border, sample tasks per stage (capped at 3, '+N more' tail), and the template's total duration, stage count, task count (AC-TB-2). 'Use this template' triggers the applyTemplate server action which copies stages, tasks, and materials into the user's project with sequential date scheduling (AC-TB-3): buildTemplateInsertPlan walks a cursor day-by-day starting at project.start_date, planned_end = planned_start + duration_days, next task planned_start = prev planned_end + 1. order_by_date = planned_start - lead_time_days for each material. Server-side summarisation keeps the browser payload small and render under 2s (AC-TB-4). After apply, /setup detects existing stages for the project and shows a 'Template applied' holding view (customization story will refine). 12 new unit tests covering insert plan computation + template summary; all 43 suite tests pass; build + typecheck clean; production deploy smoke-tested (307 → /login on /setup, auth proxy firing).

ProjectCreationForm client component with React 19 useTransition for pending state. Pure validation helpers in lib/projects/validate.ts (name required, start_date required + past-date warning with explicit checkbox confirmation, valid date parsing). localStorage draft persistence under versioned key 'cm:project-creation-draft:v1' — restores name/address/start_date on remount, cleared after successful submit. Server action app/actions/projects.ts re-runs validation server-side, inserts Project with user_id from authenticated session and status='setup', revalidates /, redirects to /setup. /setup page routes: no project → creation form; status='setup' → template browser placeholder (next story); status='active' → /briefing. Root / route already handled AC-PS-4 (redirect based on project presence/status) from foundation. Warm earth-tone design tokens applied (clay primary, amber warning, sage accents). 13 unit tests covering all 5 ACs. Total suite: 34 tests passing.

12 tables: projects, stages, trades, tasks, task_dependencies, materials, photos, documents, push_subscriptions, notification_preferences, shift_alerts, templates. RLS on all tables. cascade_task_dates() PostgreSQL function (recursive CTE). 14 performance indexes including partial index on materials.order_by_date WHERE order_status = 'not_ordered'. set_updated_at() trigger on all mutable tables. Storage buckets: photos (10MB, image/*) and documents (25MB, pdf/docx/xlsx). 4 migrations pushed to joxusnbbsoqkoocwztmz (eu-west-1). Templates seeded with 3 Irish residential builds (Standard Timber Frame 365d, Slab-on-Grade 280d, ICF Two-Storey 390d). Seed data: O'Brien Timber Frame House, 7 stages, 22 tasks, 10 materials, 3 trades.

Server client (createClient via @supabase/ssr + cookies()), browser singleton client, proxy.ts for Next.js 16 session refresh middleware (replaces middleware.ts). Login/signup/forgot-password pages with warm earth-tone styling. Server action for signout. Root redirect: unauthenticated→/login, no project→/setup, active project→/briefing.

photos bucket (10MB, jpeg/png/webp/heic, private) and documents bucket (25MB, pdf/docx/xlsx/images, private). RLS policies: authenticated users can INSERT/SELECT/DELETE. Signed URL pattern for downloads (60s expiry). setup-buckets.ts helper for programmatic bucket creation if needed.

Custom service worker (not next-pwa — removed as webpack-only incompatible with Next.js 16 Turbopack). Network-first with cache fallback for app routes. Cache-first for /_next/static/ immutable assets. Supabase API: network-only. Offline shell caches /briefing and /materials. Push + notificationclick handlers. Web App Manifest with shortcuts (Briefing, Schedule). sw.js served with no-cache header via vercel.json. ServiceWorkerRegister client component for registration.

VAPID keys generated and stored in .env.local + Vercel env vars. POST /api/push/subscribe saves subscription to push_subscriptions. DELETE unsubscribes. GET /api/cron/daily-check: Vercel Cron (7am daily), sends push for overdue tasks and upcoming order deadlines, respects notification_preferences. Cron protected by CRON_SECRET. webpush.setVapidDetails moved inside handler (not module level) to avoid Turbopack module evaluation error.

Warm earth-tone design tokens: --color-primary #8B5E3C (clay), --color-secondary #87A96B (sage), --color-accent #D4A355 (amber), --color-bg #FAF7F2 (warm off-white). AppShell: fixed sidebar (240px, desktop ≥768px) with primary + secondary nav, sign-out form action. Mobile: fixed bottom tab bar (56px, 4 primary routes). Placeholder pages for all routes with skeleton loading pattern. EmptyState, Skeleton, CardSkeleton, ErrorBoundary components. lucide-react icons throughout.

Vercel project prj_lzIoA2gwFxNGDD1Zacc1ItuF2QXM (construction-coordinator). All 7 env vars set in Vercel dashboard (NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY, VAPID_PRIVATE_KEY, VAPID_SUBJECT, CRON_SECRET). Deployed to production: https://construction-coordinator.vercel.app. Hobby tier: production deploys only (--prod flag).

Realistic Irish construction seed: O'Brien Timber Frame House, 42 Laragh Lane, Wicklow. 7 stages (Foundation→Finishes), 22 tasks with realistic dependencies (excavation→footings→slab→frame→roof→etc.), 10 materials with lead times (Ready Mix Concrete 3d, Timber Frame Kit 70d, Windows 42d, MVHR 28d, Slates 21d). 3 trades: structural engineer, groundworks contractor, timber frame supplier.

Pure trade-validation + phone-formatting in lib/trades/operations.ts (validateTradeInput for name/specialty/phone/email with optional-email regex, formatTelHref strips non-digits preserving a leading +, buildTradeDeleteWarning quotes the assignment count). Server actions (createTrade/updateTrade/deleteTrade/assignTradeToTask) verify project ownership + trade-belongs-to-project on every mutation. deleteTrade recounts assignments server-side (defense in depth) before nulling task.trade_id for all assigned tasks and deleting the trade row. /trades: server component loads trades + counts assignments from tasks.trade_id in one read each, renders the TradesManager client component with add/edit modal, tap-to-call + mailto on each row, and an explicit delete-confirmation dialog for non-empty trades. /tasks/:id: replaced the foundation placeholder with a real task detail (breadcrumb to stage, name, planned dates, notes) + a TaskTradeAssigner that re-assigns on select change with optimistic client state and a rollback on server error. 18 unit tests covering all 4 ACs. Total suite: 129 tests passing.

Pure materialDeadlineStatus(material, today) returns overdue | due_soon | null — only for not_ordered materials, using UTC day-diff to avoid TZ drift (AC-OB-2 / AC-OB-3). selectUpcomingOrders(materials, today) filters to not_ordered with order_by_date <= today+7, sorted ascending — naturally placing overdue not_ordered items at the top (AC-OB-4). MaterialDeadlineBadge shared component renders red overdue / amber due-soon pills using the warm earth-tone palette. /materials replaces placeholder skeletons with a project-scoped list joined through tasks(project_id), showing each material with its task link, order-by date, lead time, quantity, and deadline badge. /briefing replaces Today Tasks / Upcoming Orders / Shift Alerts skeletons: Upcoming Orders section now renders selectUpcomingOrders output with per-row task links. AC-OB-1 (computeOrderByDate 2026-06-15 - 14d = 2026-06-01) was already satisfied by material-line-items; added an explicit test annotation for coverage. 16 new unit tests; full suite 183 passing; typecheck + eslint clean.

nextStatusTransition (not_ordered→ordered→delivered, terminal after delivered) and filterMaterialsByStatus (all/not_ordered/ordered/delivered) added as pure functions to lib/materials/operations.ts. updateMaterialStatus server action validates one-way transitions, re-verifies task-ownership via verifyTaskOwnership, revalidates /tasks/:id and /materials. MaterialStatusBadge (ordered=green outline #EAF2E3/#3E5A2E, delivered=solid #3E5A2E) added alongside the existing deadline badge — status badge replaces the deadline badge once a material leaves not_ordered (per AC-MS-1 'green ordered badge replaces any deadline badges'). TaskMaterialsManager gains a Mark Ordered / Mark Delivered button per row (only when a transition is available) with optimistic client update + server rollback on error. /materials rebuilt around MaterialsListView client component — tab bar (All / Not Ordered / Ordered / Delivered) with per-tab counts, useMemo-memoized filter, shows status or deadline badge depending on order_status.

Story spec in state.json carried no acceptance criteria — Builder defined AC-CMS-1..4 from story name (Cascade Material Order-By Sync) and affected_screens (/materials, /briefing). AC-CMS-1: new pure helper computeMaterialMovements(materials, taskNewStarts) in lib/cascade/engine.ts computes new order_by_date = new planned_start − lead_time_days for every material attached to a cascade-touched task, mirroring the SQL cascade_task_dates() UPDATE materials branch byte-for-byte (shares computeOrderByDate math). AC-CMS-2: updateTaskDuration and logTaskDelay now return MaterialMovement[] + materials_moved count. Server actions load material metadata (id/name/lead_time_days/task_id) via loadCascadeMaterials, build an old/new planned_start map per task_id from the RPC response (trigger + downstream), and call buildMaterialMovements to assemble the per-material old_order_by_date → new_order_by_date list with delta_days. Count excludes zero-delta trigger-task materials. AC-CMS-3/AC-CMS-4: both server actions now revalidatePath("/materials") and revalidatePath("/briefing") so the affected RSCs re-render with the updated order_by_date values. TaskDelayLogger overlay renders a second list below the task list: Material order-by dates moved — one row per moved material with old → new order_by_date, delta days, and parent task name (filtered to non-zero deltas). 7 new pure unit tests cover date arithmetic, skip-when-task-not-touched, null old_order_by_date, negative deltas, and zero-lead-time edge cases. Total suite: 228 tests passing. Typecheck + lint clean.

AC-DV-1: Pure detectCycle(edges, proposed) uses DFS from the proposed dependency target, following existing depends_on edges. If the traversal reaches the proposing task, a cycle exists. formatCyclePath renders the cycle as 'This would create a circular dependency: A → B → A' using task names (falls back to IDs). Server-side: addDependency server action loads all project dependency edges, runs detectCycle, rejects with formatted error if cycle found. removeDependency action for unchecking deps. StageTasksManager adds a 'Deps' button per task opening a modal with checkboxes for all project tasks — toggling a checkbox calls addDependency/removeDependency, with inline error display (data-testid=dep-cycle-error) for cycle rejections. AC-DV-2: stages/[id] page RSC loads raw dependency edges, runs removeStaleDependencies(edges, validProjectTaskIds), deletes stale edges from task_dependencies table, passes only cleaned edges to the client. Also cleanStaleDependencies server action exposed for programmatic use. AC-DV-3: DFS is O(V+E) — unit tests verify cycle detection on 100-task chain, 200-task fan-out graph, and 500-task chain all complete under 500ms. 18 new unit tests across cycle detection (empty graph, direct cycle, transitive cycle, self-dep, fan-out, long chain), formatCyclePath (names, fallback IDs, 3-node), removeStaleDependencies (stale target, stale source, all valid, empty), and performance benchmarks. Total suite: 246 tests passing. Typecheck clean.

AC-DB-1: /briefing now renders three sections — Today's Tasks, Upcoming Orders, Shift Alerts — each in a card with warm earth-tone styling matching the design system. AC-DB-2: selectTodayTasks pure function filters tasks where planned_start <= today <= planned_end AND status IN ('not_started', 'in_progress'), sorted by planned_start ASC then name ASC. Server component queries tasks joined through stages.project_id. AC-DB-3: When no tasks match today, empty state shows 'No tasks scheduled today — next task starts [date]' with a link to /schedule. nextTaskStartDate finds the earliest future planned_start among active tasks. If no future tasks exist, shows 'View schedule' link instead. AC-DB-4: Mobile-first layout — max-w-2xl container, sections stack vertically with mb-4 spacing, all text truncates via Tailwind truncate class, no horizontal overflow. Bottom tab bar inherited from AppShell layout. AC-DB-5: RefreshButton client component (~1KB) uses useRouter().refresh() + useTransition for loading state. Spinner animates during refresh. Positioned in the page header. AC-DB-6: Server component with zero client JS for data sections. Promise.all parallelizes the 3 DB queries (tasks, materials, shift_alerts). Only client component is RefreshButton. Build output confirms /briefing is ƒ Dynamic (server-rendered on demand). 18 new unit tests in tests/briefing-operations.test.ts covering selectTodayTasks (8 tests: date range filtering, status filtering, boundary cases, sorting, empty input), nextTaskStartDate (5 tests: earliest future, no future, excludes complete/delayed, empty, today boundary), formatShiftAlert (3 tests: date_moved, null old_value, status_changed), shiftAlertHref (2 tests: task → /tasks/:id, material → /materials). Total suite: 264 tests passing. Typecheck + ESLint + production build clean.

Task Quick Actions on /briefing. AC-QA-1: checkbox per task row toggles status to complete via toggleTaskComplete server action (verifies project ownership, sets actual_end=today, revalidates /briefing+/tasks/:id+/schedule). Row visually strikes through with line-through class and muted text color. AC-QA-2: tapping completed checkbox reverts to in_progress via computeToggleResult pure fn (complete→in_progress, actualEnd→null). BriefingTaskList uses optimistic state so the toggle is instant, then router.refresh() syncs RSC truth. selectTodayTasks updated to include complete tasks (sorted after incomplete) so toggled tasks remain visible for undo. AC-QA-3: desktop kebab menu (three-dot button, outside-click dismissal) shows View Detail, Log Delay, Add Note — each links to /tasks/:id with hash anchors. Mobile: swipe left (60px threshold via touchStart/touchEnd tracking) slides the row to reveal three colored action buttons (Detail=clay, Log Delay=amber, Add Note=sage). Swipe right or outside tap dismisses. AC-QA-4: optimistic UI + useTransition wrapping toggleTaskComplete + router.refresh() updates the briefing in place without full page reload. Quick action links navigate to /tasks/:id where Log Delay and other actions already exist from prior stories. 6 new unit tests (4 for computeToggleResult, 2 for selectTodayTasks with complete-task inclusion). 270 total tests passing. Typecheck + production build clean (19 routes, Proxy middleware detected).

Upcoming Material Orders View on /briefing. AC-UO-1: replaced plain list with horizontally-scrollable cards on mobile (flex + overflow-x-auto, min-w-[220px] per card) and responsive grid on desktop (md:grid-cols-2, lg:grid-cols-3). AC-UO-2: each card shows material name, associated task name, days remaining until order_by_date (computed by daysUntilOrderBy pure helper using existing daysBetweenIso), and supplier_name when set. Days label: "Xd left" / "Due today" / "Xd overdue" with color-coded text (red for overdue, amber for <=2d). AC-UO-3: tap/click toggles inline expansion below the card showing quantity, estimated_cost (formatted via formatMaterialCost as EUR with Intl.NumberFormat), lead_time_days with singular/plural, notes, and a "Mark Ordered" button that calls the existing updateMaterialStatus server action (not_ordered→ordered transition) then router.refresh(). Only one card expanded at a time (accordion pattern). AC-UO-4: entire Upcoming Orders section conditionally rendered — hidden when upcomingCards.length === 0, no empty state message shown. Briefing page material query extended to fetch quantity, estimated_cost, lead_time_days, supplier_name, notes alongside existing fields. 13 pure unit tests (daysUntilOrderBy: future/today/overdue/null/malformed/month-boundary; formatMaterialCost: formatted/zero/null/whole; selectUpcomingOrders: empty/ordered-excluded/overdue-sorting). 8 component tests (card rendering: name/task/days/supplier/overdue-label/due-today/no-supplier; expand: hidden-by-default/expand-shows-all-fields/collapse-on-second-click/singular-day). 291 total tests passing. Typecheck + production build clean.

AC-SA-1: Pure functions buildTaskShiftAlerts(movements, projectId, userId) and buildMaterialShiftAlerts(movements, projectId, userId) in lib/briefing/shift-alerts.ts convert cascade TaskMovement[] and MaterialMovement[] into ShiftAlertInsert rows. Each task with changed planned_end gets a date_moved alert (old_value=old planned_end, new_value=new planned_end). Each material with non-zero delta_days gets a date_moved alert (old/new order_by_date). Zero-delta and null-delta entries are skipped. Both updateTaskDuration and logTaskDelay server actions now call insertShiftAlerts() after computing the cascade summary, persisting shift_alert rows for every moved entity. Best-effort — a failed insert does not block the cascade result. AC-SA-2: Already implemented by existing formatShiftAlert ("[name] moved from [old] to [new]") and shiftAlertHref (task→/tasks/:id, material→/materials) from the daily-briefing-view story. The briefing page renders each alert as a clickable Link. AC-SA-3: dismissShiftAlerts server action in app/actions/briefing.ts sets dismissed=true on all non-dismissed shift_alerts matching the user + project. DismissAlertsButton client component (~1KB) in the Shift Alerts section header shows "Clear" when alerts.length > 0, calls dismissShiftAlerts via useTransition + router.refresh(). After dismissal, only alerts created after the dismiss timestamp appear (because the query filters dismissed=false). 7 new unit tests. 298 total passing. Typecheck + production build clean.

Push notification subscription management for /settings. AC-PN-1: ServiceWorkerRegistrar client component registers /sw.js on app load in (app)/layout.tsx — silently fails if browser lacks SW support. AC-PN-2: PushNotificationToggle on /settings detects browser push capability via detectPushCapability (pure fn testing hasSW, hasPush, Notification.permission), requests Notification.requestPermission() on first enable, subscribes via PushManager.subscribe() with VAPID applicationServerKey (urlBase64ToUint8Array converts base64url to Uint8Array), serializes the subscription (serializeSubscription extracts endpoint + p256dh + auth), and POSTs to /api/push/subscribe to persist in push_subscriptions. Toggle reflects enabled (sage green switch). AC-PN-3: Disable path unsubscribes via PushSubscription.unsubscribe() then DELETEs to /api/push/subscribe to mark is_active=false. Toggle reflects disabled state. AC-PN-4: If Notification.permission is "denied", toggle shows "Notifications Blocked" disabled state with amber hint text instructing user to enable in browser settings. "Not Supported" state for browsers without PushManager. AC-PN-5: ensureNotificationPreferences server action on /settings load upserts a notification_preferences row with defaults (order_deadlines=true, overdue_tasks=true, cascade_summaries=true, order_warning_days=3) — fixes foundation finding where cron INNER JOIN silently excluded users without prefs. Settings page renders current preference state (On/Off badges) for material orders, overdue tasks, cascade summaries. deriveToggleState pure fn maps (capability, hasSubscription) to UI state (label, enabled, disabled, showBlockedHint). 19 new unit tests: 6 for detectPushCapability (unsupported SW, unsupported Push, default/denied/granted/null permission), 5 for deriveToggleState (unsupported/blocked/enable/disable/re-subscribe), 4 for serializeSubscription (valid/missing-p256dh/missing-auth/missing-keys), 4 for urlBase64ToUint8Array (known vectors, padding, url-safe chars, round-trip). Total suite: 317 tests passing. Typecheck + production build clean (19 routes).

Builder-defined ACs (spec was id+name only). AC-NS-1: Each boolean preference (order_deadlines, overdue_tasks, cascade_summaries) now has a toggle switch (role=switch, aria-checked) that calls toggleNotificationPreference server action — reads current value, flips it, writes back, revalidates /settings. Styled consistently with PushNotificationToggle (sage green #87A96B on, muted #D4C5B5 off). AC-NS-2: order_warning_days has a +/- stepper (buttons with aria-labels) clamped to 1–14 matching the DB CHECK constraint. Each click calls updateOrderWarningDays server action with server-side validateWarningDays re-validation. Current value displayed between buttons in tabular-nums for stable layout. The order_deadlines row description dynamically reflects the current warning days count with correct singular/plural. AC-NS-3: Both toggles and stepper use optimistic state via useState + useTransition. The UI updates instantly; if the server action fails, the previous state is restored and an error message appears via role=alert. Buttons show opacity-50 + cursor-not-allowed while the transition is pending. AC-NS-4: validateWarningDays rejects non-integer, non-finite, out-of-range (0, 15, -1), empty string, NaN, Infinity. Stepper buttons are disabled at bounds (min=1, max=14) preventing invalid submissions. Server action double-checks via the same validator. 23 new unit tests in tests/notification-preferences.test.ts covering validateWarningDays (12 cases: valid range ends, mid-range, string input, zero, negative, above-max, decimal, non-numeric string, empty, NaN, Infinity), togglePreference (5 cases: each key true→false, false→true, immutability), updateWarningDays (2 cases: value change, immutability), PREFERENCE_LABELS (2 cases: key completeness, field presence), constants (2 cases). Total suite: 340 tests passing. Typecheck + production build clean (19 routes, Proxy middleware).

Extracted BriefingHeader component from inline JSX in briefing/page.tsx. Component renders h1 "Daily Briefing" with project.name as a text-sm text-[#6B5D52] subtitle below. AC-PI-1: project name rendered in header region via BriefingHeader prop. AC-PI-2: p element with text-sm class follows the h1 heading. AC-PI-3: truncate class on the subtitle prevents overflow for long project names; container is already max-w-2xl. RefreshButton moved into BriefingHeader, unused import removed from page.tsx. 3 new component tests using @testing-library/react. Full suite: 353 tests passing. Build clean (19 routes).

Gantt chart on /schedule. Pure computation in lib/gantt/compute.ts: computeDateRange (1-day buffer each side), computeTaskBars (position by planned_start offset, width = duration_days, color from stage, row assignment by stage order_index then task order_index), computeDependencyArrows (end-of-from to start-of-to with graceful skip for missing deps), daysBetween (UTC-safe calendar day diff). AC-GR-1: Desktop (>=1024px) renders horizontal time axis with day columns in DateHeader and vertical stage groups. AC-GR-2: TaskBarEl positions each bar by startDay * DAY_WIDTH, width = widthDays * DAY_WIDTH, backgroundColor = stage.color. AC-GR-3: DependencyArrowsSvg draws SVG paths with arrowhead markers from dependency end to dependent start. AC-GR-4: Completed tasks (status='complete') render with opacity 0.65 and checkmark overlay. AC-GR-5: Delayed tasks (actual_end > planned_end) render with ring-2 ring-red-500 and '+Nd' badge. AC-GR-6: Mobile (<1024px) MobileGanttView shows stages-only accordion; tapping a stage expands to show its task bars with horizontal scroll. /schedule page now fetches tasks (with planned_start, planned_end, duration_days, status, actual_end) and task_dependencies, builds GanttTask[] with depends_on arrays, passes to GanttChart alongside GanttStage[]. StageManager remains below Gantt for stage CRUD. 16 pure unit tests + 11 component tests. Full suite: 380 tests passing. Typecheck + production build clean (19 routes).

Gantt zoom and navigation. AC-GZ-1: Three zoom buttons (Week/Month/Full) in a toolbar above the Gantt. Week = 28px/day daily grid (default), Month = 8px/day weekly grid with week-start date labels, Full = 3px/day monthly grid with month/year labels. Buttons use aria-pressed for accessibility, active button styled with clay primary (#8B5E3C). Pure getZoomConfig(zoom) in lib/gantt/compute.ts returns dayWidth and headerMode per level. DateHeader renders daily columns, weekly spans (grouped to Monday boundaries), or monthly spans (grouped to 1st of month). Labels hidden when span width < 30px to prevent text overflow. AC-GZ-2: Today marker (red vertical line with "Today" label) already existed from gantt-chart-render — verified present and working at all zoom levels. AC-GZ-3: Horizontal scroll via overflow-x-auto on the desktop container — trackpad, mouse wheel, and touch swipe all work natively via CSS. No re-rendering on scroll (CSS-only panning). AC-GZ-4: "Jump to Today" button calls computeTodayOffset (pure fn) to get today pixel position, then scrollTo with behavior:smooth to center today in the viewport. 3 new pure tests (getZoomConfig configs, computeTodayOffset offsets/null for out-of-range). 5 new component tests (toolbar renders, default active state, zoom toggle, width changes on zoom, jump-today button present). 393 total tests passing. Production build clean (19 routes).

Gantt inline editing with four acceptance criteria. AC-GE-1: InteractiveTaskBar detects mousedown on the right 8px of a bar as a resize drag. On mouseup, computeResizeResult (pure fn in lib/gantt/drag.ts) computes new duration from pixel position, updateTaskDuration server action fires cascade_task_dates RPC. Duration clamped to minimum 1 day. AC-GE-2: Mousedown on the bar body initiates a move drag. computeMoveResult computes new start/end from pixel delta. On release, validateTaskDrop checks dependency constraints client-side; if valid, moveTaskDates server action (new) calls cascade_task_dates with both new planned_start and planned_end, propagates to downstream tasks + materials atomically. AC-GE-3: Sub-4px mouse movement is treated as a click, opening TaskDetailPanel — a right-side slide-in panel (200ms ease-out CSS animation) showing task name, stage color chip + name, start/end dates, duration, status badge, trade name, materials list, dependency count, and an Edit Task button linking to /tasks/:id. Closes on Escape key, click outside, or close button. AC-GE-4: validateTaskDrop (pure fn) computes the minimum start day from dependency end dates. If the drop position is before any dependency completes, the bar snaps back (state cleared) and a red toast with 'Cannot start before dependency completes' appears for 4 seconds. 20 pure unit tests in gantt-drag.test.ts. 8 component tests in gantt-chart.test.tsx. All 420 tests passing. Typecheck + production build clean.

Builder-defined ACs (spec was id+name only). AC-PS-1: Photo upload on /tasks/:id via file picker with capture="environment" for mobile camera. Client validates MIME type (jpeg/png/webp/heic) and 10MB size limit via validatePhotoFile pure fn before upload. FormData sent to uploadPhoto server action which stores file in Supabase Storage "photos" bucket at {project_id}/{entity_id}/{timestamp}-{sanitized_name} path, then inserts metadata row in photos table (project_id, task_id, stage_id, storage_path, file_name, file_size). Cleanup: if metadata insert fails, storage object is removed. AC-PS-2: TaskPhotosManager client component renders photo grid (2 cols mobile, 3 cols sm) on /tasks/:id below materials section. Each thumbnail shows signed URL preview (60s expiry), file_name, date (formatPhotoDate with en-IE locale), file_size (formatFileSize). Delete button (X overlay on hover/focus) per photo. Optimistic add/remove with router.refresh() sync. AC-PS-3: /photos gallery server component loads all project photos, generates batch signed URLs via createSignedUrls, groups by stage using groupPhotosByStage pure fn (stages in order_index order, unassigned group at end, photos sorted most-recent first within each group). Each group shows stage color chip + name + count. Photo cards link to /tasks/:id. Empty state with link to /schedule. AC-PS-4: deletePhoto server action verifies project ownership, removes storage object first, then deletes metadata row. Revalidates /tasks/:id, /stages/:id, and /photos. AC-PS-5: Client-side validatePhotoFile rejects non-image MIME types with specific error message and files over 10MB with size display. Error shown via role="alert" element. 24 pure unit tests (validatePhotoFile: 8 MIME/size cases; buildStoragePath: 3 path/sanitize cases; formatFileSize: 5 cases; formatPhotoDate: 3 cases; groupPhotosByStage: 5 cases). 8 component tests (empty state, thumbnails, count, add button, no-preview fallback, image rendering, delete buttons, camera capture attribute). 452 total tests passing. Typecheck + production build clean (19 routes).

Document upload & storage on /tasks/:id and /stages/:id. Pure operations module (lib/documents/operations.ts) with validateDocumentFile (25MB cap per AC-DS-4 with exact spec error message, MIME allowlist for PDF/JPEG/PNG/DOCX/XLSX), buildDocumentStoragePath (same convention as photos: project/entity/timestamp-sanitized_name), formatFileSize, formatUploadDate, fileTypeIcon (maps MIME → pdf/word/excel/image/file for AC-DS-2 icon rendering). Server actions (app/actions/documents.ts): uploadDocument (AC-DS-1 — FormData upload to Supabase Storage documents bucket + metadata insert with project ownership verification, cleanup on insert failure), getDocumentDownloadUrl (AC-DS-3 — signed URL with 60s expiry), deleteDocument (storage + metadata removal). TaskDocumentsManager client component renders document rows as a list (not a grid like photos) with per-row file type icon, file name, upload date, file size, Download button, and delete X. Reuses the same warm earth-tone design tokens. Wired into /tasks/:id (task-scoped documents with stage_id passthrough) and /stages/:id (stage-scoped documents where task_id is null). 23 pure unit tests + 7 component tests = 30 new tests. 482 total suite passing. TypeScript clean. Production build clean (20 routes). Deployed to https://construction-coordinator.vercel.app (HTTP 307 → /login confirmed).

Export button on /settings (AC-EX-1) opens a format selection dialog with three options: JSON, CSV, PDF. JSON export (AC-EX-2): fetchExportData server action loads all project entities (project, stages, tasks, materials, trades, photos, documents) with auth ownership check, then buildJsonExport assembles the export object stripping photo/document binary fields (keeping metadata + storage URLs). serializeJsonExport produces pretty-printed JSON. CSV export (AC-EX-3): buildStagesCsv/buildTasksCsv/buildMaterialsCsv/buildTradesCsv produce individual CSVs with human-readable headers and proper escaping (commas, quotes, newlines). buildZip assembles a valid ZIP archive (STORE method, no compression) with zero external dependencies — custom implementation using local file headers + central directory + EOCD record with CRC-32 checksums. PDF export (AC-EX-4): uses window.print() for browser-native print-to-PDF — see divergence. ExportDialog client component: format selection with aria-pressed radio-style buttons, loading state via useTransition, error display, backdrop close. ExportButton wraps the dialog trigger for use in the server-rendered /settings page. 23 pure-logic unit tests (JSON assembly, CSV escaping, CSV builders, ZIP structure verification) + 11 component tests (dialog open/close, format selection, AC coverage). 516 total tests passing. Typecheck + production build clean (19 routes).

skipped.reason: Vercel CLI not installed in this environment (system-reminder flagged); project deploys via vercel push which is outside the autonomous loop's tool surface. QA phase will detect missing deploy if one is required.
skipped.unblocked_by: Install vercel CLI (npm i -g vercel) or run deploy from the outer loop

skipped.task: AC-DL-4 offline queue + Delay synced toast
skipped.reason: No client-side offline action queue infrastructure exists yet. The sw.js service worker is registered but has no Background Sync or IndexedDB replay logic.
skipped.unblocked_by: An offline-sync story that adds an IndexedDB mutation queue + Background Sync API registration + toast subscriber for queued-action results.

divergences.actually_did: Custom hand-written service worker at public/sw.js
divergences.rationale: next-pwa v5 is webpack-only and incompatible with Next.js 16 Turbopack (default bundler). Custom service worker is actually simpler, has no Workbox dependency, and is fully portable. Push and notification handlers work identically.

divergences.actually_did: proxy.ts with proxy() function and proxyConfig export
divergences.rationale: Next.js 16 renamed middleware.ts to proxy.ts. Confirmed by Vercel platform context. The ƒ Proxy (Middleware) entry in the build output confirms it is correctly detected.

divergences.spec_says: AI assist chat panel should return suggestions for free-form descriptions (e.g. 'I'm doing ICF foundation not traditional pour')
divergences.actually_did: Implemented a deterministic keyword-matched suggester (ICF, insulation, solar) behind the same suggestFromMessage(message, snapshot) interface a real LLM call would use
divergences.rationale: The project has no Anthropic SDK, OpenAI SDK, or API key wiring — shipping a real LLM call would require Vercel env-var plumbing, secret handling, streaming handling, and error/retry logic that belongs in its own story. The rule-based stub demonstrates the accept/reject flow end-to-end for the three seeded template archetypes and can be swapped for a real Claude API call later by replacing the body of suggestFromMessage. This is documented as an env_limitation on the story result.

divergences.spec_says: AC-DB-5: pull down to refresh (mobile) or clicks refresh (desktop)
divergences.actually_did: Refresh button on both mobile and desktop. No native pull-down-to-refresh gesture.
divergences.rationale: Native pull-to-refresh requires either a touch event handler with rubber-band physics or a third-party library. The Refresh button using router.refresh() + useTransition provides the same data reload capability on both form factors. The native gesture can be added in a follow-up without changing the data flow.

divergences.spec_says: AC-DB-2: selectTodayTasks filters to status IN (not_started, in_progress)
divergences.actually_did: selectTodayTasks now also includes status=complete tasks within the date range
divergences.rationale: AC-QA-1/AC-QA-2 require toggling completion on the briefing and undoing it. If complete tasks were excluded, checking a task would remove it from the list instantly, preventing undo. Complete tasks sort after incomplete ones to keep the active work prominent.

divergences.spec_says: AC-EX-4: a PDF snapshot of the current Gantt chart (full project zoom) is rendered and downloaded
divergences.actually_did: PDF export triggers window.print() which opens the browser print dialog. The user can select Save as PDF to download. No automatic PDF file generation.
divergences.rationale: Generating a true PDF programmatically would require adding html2canvas (~400KB) + jspdf (~300KB) — two new dependencies for a single feature. The browser print dialog produces higher-quality output with native PDF rendering. McKinley principle: spend innovation tokens on the core differentiator (export data portability via JSON/CSV), use boring technology (browser print) for the visual snapshot. The Gantt is DOM-based, not canvas-based, so print CSS can style it appropriately.

factory_decisions.decision: AI Assist implemented as a deterministic keyword-matched suggester (not a live LLM call)
factory_decisions.context: AC-TC-3 specifies 'the AI suggests specific task/material additions or substitutions with accept/reject buttons' and notes 'Requires LLM integration (Claude API or similar)'. The project has no Anthropic/OpenAI SDK in package.json, no API key in .env.local, and no server-side LLM helper.
alternatives_considered: Add @anthropic-ai/sdk + ANTHROPIC_API_KEY + streaming response handling within this story — doubles the story scope, introduces a new secret to manage, and conflates LLM infra work with the UX story
Stub with a random/static suggestion regardless of input — fails AC-TC-3's specificity requirement
Skip AC-TC-3 entirely — leaves the chat panel un-demoable and blocks AC-TC-4
factory_decisions.rationale: suggestFromMessage(message, snapshot) is a pure function with a stable interface — it returns AssistantSuggestion | null. A keyword matcher (ICF → toggle off slab + add ICF wall; insulation → add upgrade; solar → add PV) produces correct, specific, testable output for the three seeded templates. The whole chat UI, accept/reject flow, applySuggestion merger, and downstream applyCustomization are all real and exercised by the stub. The LLM swap is a one-function body replacement in a future llm-integration story.

factory_decisions.decision: Broken-dependency warning fires on DIRECT deps only, not transitive closure
factory_decisions.context: AC-TC-2: 'any downstream tasks that depended on it show a warning icon'. Could mean direct depends_on rows only, or full transitive closure (A→B→C, toggle A, flag both B and C).
alternatives_considered: Transitive closure via BFS over task_dependencies — O(V+E) per render, correct but hides the actual breakage point from the user
Flag nothing (leave cascade function to handle) — violates AC-TC-2 explicitly
Flag every task after the toggled task in order_index — ignores real deps, produces noise
factory_decisions.rationale: Direct-dep flagging has O(deps) complexity and shows the user exactly where a dep broke. As the user toggles off B (now flagged), C becomes flagged next — so transitive breakage surfaces naturally through iterative toggling rather than a single noisy warning cloud. Dormant in this story since template-apply doesn't write task_dependencies rows yet, but the UI is correct and tested for when deps are added in the task-management cycle.

factory_decisions.decision: Customization state held in client (React useState) until 'Create project' is clicked
factory_decisions.context: User toggles tasks + accepts suggestions across multiple interactions. Could persist optimistically on each change, or batch all changes into the final submit.
alternatives_considered: Persist every toggle via server action — N round trips, chat feels sluggish, partial-state DB rows if user abandons
localStorage draft like project-creation-draft — extra layer of state sync bugs for a flow the user finishes in one sitting
Supabase Realtime subscriptions for multi-device edit — overkill for single-user setup phase
factory_decisions.rationale: Customization is a single focused step on one device ending in 'Create project'. Client-only state means zero round trips during the customization itself, one round trip on submit (applyCustomization), one redirect (finalizeProject). If the user abandons, no partial DB state. Matches the browser-only draft pattern from TemplateBrowser.

factory_decisions.decision: Added tasks get planned_start = project.start_date on insert (not scheduled inline)
factory_decisions.context: applyCustomization inserts new tasks mid-schedule. Could: (a) recompute the cursor-based schedule across all tasks, (b) insert at project.start_date and let cascade_task_dates() refine, (c) compute a new planned_start based on the stage's existing task range.
alternatives_considered: Rebuild the cursor schedule for all tasks (delete+reinsert) — heavy, risks losing user-edited dates from later stories
Place new task inline at max(planned_end) within the stage — requires a pre-insert SELECT and still doesn't form proper dependencies
Leave planned_start null — violates tasks.planned_start NOT NULL schema constraint
factory_decisions.rationale: Start-date placeholder + cascade refinement is the pattern the schema was designed for: cascade_task_dates() is the single source of truth for date propagation. This story doesn't need to reinvent scheduling. The user can drag dates in the schedule-management story and cascade will propagate. NOT NULL satisfied; dates self-correct on first cascade.

factory_decisions.decision: Sequential date scheduling (no dependency graph) for template-apply
factory_decisions.context: Templates carry stages/tasks/materials but no task-dependency info. Template-apply needs to satisfy tasks.planned_start NOT NULL + planned_end NOT NULL on insert. Options: leave dates null (rejected by schema), chain linearly, or attempt to infer dependencies from task order.
alternatives_considered: Defer date computation to a separate cascade step (breaks schema NOT NULL constraints; would need migration)
Template carries explicit depends_on — requires reshaping seed JSONB and template-customization story handles dependency editing already
All tasks share project.start_date — loses the ability to show a meaningful schedule in the Gantt before customization
factory_decisions.rationale: Cursor-based linear schedule: planned_start of task N = planned_end of task N-1 + 1 day, planned_end = planned_start + duration_days. Produces a reasonable initial Gantt immediately after template apply; the user edits durations and dependencies in the template-customization + task-management stories and cascade_task_dates() refines from there. Pure function keeps the scheduling logic unit-testable without DB round-trips.

factory_decisions.decision: Server-side template summarization (summarizeTemplate) — send TemplateSummary to the browser, not the full JSONB
factory_decisions.context: Each template stores a fat stages JSONB (whole build with materials). Sending all 3 to the client for list + preview would waste ~15KB and block the 2s render target in AC-TB-4.
alternatives_considered: Ship raw templates array, summarize in the client — duplicates logic + bloats JS bundle
Paginate / lazy-load preview — overkill for 3 seeded templates
Render preview server-side with server actions — adds round-trip on every card click
factory_decisions.rationale: /setup computes TemplateSummary (stage/task counts, sample_tasks per stage, total_duration_days with computed fallback) in the RSC, passes only summaries to the TemplateBrowser client component. Apply still reads the full JSONB server-side via templateId. Keeps client payload small, server logic centralized, and preview switching instant.

factory_decisions.decision: Detect already-applied-template by counting stages on the project rather than a project flag
factory_decisions.context: After apply, the project is still status='setup' (customization story flips to 'active'). Navigating back to /setup needs to avoid letting the user apply a second template on top of the first.
alternatives_considered: Add a projects.template_id or projects.template_applied_at column — schema churn for a UI guard
Redirect to a dedicated /setup/customize route after apply — premature; customization story hasn't defined that route yet
Client-side localStorage flag — unreliable across devices
factory_decisions.rationale: A quick HEAD count on stages WHERE project_id = X is cheap (partial index exists), stateless, and inherently correct: if any stages exist, the template was applied. Works even if the user opened /setup on a second device. Zero schema changes.

factory_decisions.decision: Use local synthetic ids ('s0', 't0-1') in the insert plan to join inserts across three tables
factory_decisions.context: applyTemplate has to insert stages, then tasks (needing the stage's real id), then materials (needing the task's real id). supabase.insert().select() returns real ids but not in the same order as input, so a join key is required.
alternatives_considered: Insert stages one at a time to capture each id inline — N round trips instead of 1
Rely on array index ordering — Supabase does not guarantee it
Use Postgres CTE with WITH clauses — requires a stored procedure or rpc, more infra than needed
factory_decisions.rationale: Insert plan tags each row with a deterministic _localId ('s{order_index}', 't{stageIdx}-{taskIdx}'), and both stages.order_index and tasks.(stage_id + order_index) are carried through inserts. The server action rebuilds the local→real mapping from the returned rows after each insert. Three round trips total, correct by construction.

factory_decisions.context: AC-PS-1/2/3 involve branching validation (empty name, past date warning, confirmation flag). The form needs client-side validation for UX; the server action needs identical validation for safety. Two code paths = duplication risk.
alternatives_considered: Validate only server-side (requires round-trip for every validation error — poor mobile UX)
Validate only client-side (security hole — malicious clients can POST invalid data)
Schema library (zod) — another dep for 5 rules this simple
factory_decisions.rationale: Pure functions accepting (values, today) return { errors, warnings }. Used by both the form and the server action. 13 unit tests run in <10ms, no mocks needed. McKinley: boring technology — plain TypeScript, no runtime deps.

factory_decisions.decision: Past-date confirmation via checkbox inside an amber warning panel (not browser confirm())
factory_decisions.context: AC-PS-3 requires 'explicit confirmation' for past start_date. Options: native window.confirm(), inline checkbox, two-step submit.
alternatives_considered: window.confirm() — blocking modal, inaccessible, ugly on mobile
Two-step submit (first click shows warning, second submits) — confusing UX, easy to double-click through
Modal dialog — overkill for a single confirmation
factory_decisions.rationale: Inline amber panel with a checkbox: visible, accessible, preserves form context, prevents accidental submits. Confirmation state resets whenever the date changes so the user re-confirms if they adjust.

factory_decisions.decision: localStorage draft persistence stored under versioned key 'cm:project-creation-draft:v1'
factory_decisions.context: AC-PS-5: form restore across sessions. Need a key that won't collide with other drafts and can be migrated.
alternatives_considered: sessionStorage (lost on tab close, fails AC-PS-5)
IndexedDB (overkill for 3 string fields)
Unversioned key 'project-draft' (can't migrate schema without orphaning old drafts)
factory_decisions.rationale: Namespaced + versioned key allows future form schema changes. Draft is cleared after successful createProject redirect (returns to same page, effect unmounts, clearDraft called in the success path).

factory_decisions.decision: Cast-at-site for projects.insert() rather than editing lib/supabase/types.ts
factory_decisions.context: Foundation eval flagged lib/supabase/types.ts as lacking Relationships[] on every table, which causes typed Postgrest inserts to resolve to 'never'. Options: regenerate types, add Relationships: [] to all 12 tables, or cast at call sites.
alternatives_considered: Edit all 12 tables to add Relationships: [] — touches 12 tables for a single insert, high blast radius
Regenerate types via supabase gen types — correct long-term fix but requires Supabase CLI auth and migration roundtrip, out of scope for this story
Disable strict typing globally — defeats the purpose
factory_decisions.rationale: Single targeted cast in app/actions/projects.ts with a comment pointing at the foundation-eval finding. The typed Row/Insert shapes are still used by app/page.tsx and app/(app)/setup/page.tsx for reads. A dedicated types-regeneration pass belongs in a separate chore, not buried inside a feature story.

factory_decisions.decision: Removed next-pwa, wrote custom service worker
factory_decisions.context: next-pwa v5 injects webpack config, incompatible with Next.js 16 Turbopack default. Build failed with 'This build is using Turbopack, with a webpack config and no turbopack config.'
alternatives_considered: @ducanh2912/next-pwa — Turbopack-compatible fork, but adds another dependency for a problem solvable with 80 lines of vanilla JS
next build --webpack — disables Turbopack, slower builds, not idiomatic for Next.js 16
factory_decisions.rationale: A custom sw.js at public/sw.js served with no-cache headers is the recommended Next.js 16 approach. No Workbox dependency, full control over caching strategy, easier to debug.

factory_decisions.decision: Supabase project 'construction-coordinator' (joxusnbbsoqkoocwztmz) used for construction-manager
factory_decisions.context: Git branch [isolation-recovery: this decision was reverted — construction-coordinator matched both the Supabase project name and Vercel project name. ACTIVE_HEALTHY status confirmed.
alternatives_considered: Create new Supabase project — would require pausing an existing project (at 2/2 free tier limit) and waiting for activation
Use 'openfleet' project — wrong project, different product
factory_decisions.rationale: The naming alignment (construction-coordinator project + construction-coordinator Supabase + construction-coordinator Vercel project) is unambiguous. Project was pre-created as part of the build setup.

factory_decisions.decision: webpush.setVapidDetails() moved inside handler functions
factory_decisions.context: Calling setVapidDetails at module evaluation time caused build failure on Vercel: 'No key set in vapidDetails.privateKey'. Turbopack evaluates route modules during static page generation.
alternatives_considered: Lazy initialization singleton — adds unnecessary complexity
Dynamic import of web-push — works but slower
factory_decisions.rationale: Moving setVapidDetails inside the handler is the simplest fix. It runs once per cold start in production (Fluid Compute reuses instances). No performance impact.

factory_decisions.decision: cascade_task_dates() implemented as PostgreSQL function with recursive CTE
factory_decisions.context: Cascade engine spec requires single-transaction update of all downstream tasks. Acceptable: under 2s for 500 tasks.
alternatives_considered: Application-layer BFS in TypeScript — multiple round trips, not atomic, slower for large graphs
PostgreSQL stored procedure with LOOP — not set-based, harder to optimize
factory_decisions.rationale: Recursive CTE traverses the full DAG in one query, updates all affected tasks + materials atomically. PostgreSQL can optimize the traversal with the existing task_dependencies indexes.

factory_decisions.decision: shift_alerts table for briefing delta tracking
factory_decisions.context: Daily briefing needs to show 'what changed since last visit'. Options: compute on-the-fly (expensive join), event log (append-only), or client-side localStorage.
alternatives_considered: On-the-fly computation — requires tracking last_visit_at per user and joining across all cascades
localStorage — lost on device switch, not reliable for mobile
Supabase Realtime subscription — overkill for a daily summary
factory_decisions.rationale: Server-side event log (shift_alerts) written by the cascade function. Dismissed flag per user. Readable from any device. Foundation builds the table; daily-experience feature cycle builds the UI.

factory_decisions.decision: StageManager uses client-side optimistic updates with server-side reorderProjectStages as the persistence truth
factory_decisions.context: AC-SM-2 requires drag reorder to re-render the Gantt "with the new ordering." Options: (a) drag → server round trip → server returns new list → re-render; (b) client reorders locally, server action confirms in background; (c) server action re-throws errors and force-reverts.
alternatives_considered: Server-first: every drag waits for the server — drag feels laggy, breaks the direct-manipulation UX spec
Optimistic + rollback on failure: requires snapshot + restore logic; adds state management for a rare failure path
Supabase Realtime subscription: overkill for single-user project editing
factory_decisions.rationale: Drag updates the local stages state immediately (pure reorderStages produces the renumbered array), then fires reorderProjectStages in a startTransition. If the server fails, serverError banner shows and the user can retry. Single-device single-user editing means conflict is vanishingly rare; the optimistic path keeps drag feeling instant. Matches the client-only draft pattern from ProjectCreationForm + CustomizationScreen.

factory_decisions.decision: Up/down arrow buttons shipped alongside native HTML5 drag-and-drop
factory_decisions.context: AC-SM-2 says "drags a stage to reorder" but this is a PWA and touch devices (iOS Safari) do not reliably fire HTML5 drag events. The alternatives are a heavier touch-drag library (react-dnd, dnd-kit), omitting mobile support, or providing a parallel keyboard/tap interaction.
alternatives_considered: Install dnd-kit or react-dnd for unified touch+mouse drag — new dependency, ~40KB, another abstraction to learn, McKinley says no unless drag is core
Ship drag-only, leave mobile broken — violates PWA product standard; stage reorder is core enough that mobile parity matters
Polyfill TouchEvent → DragEvent — fragile, hand-rolled, adds more bugs than it solves
factory_decisions.rationale: Boring-tech drag via draggable=true covers desktop (the primary Gantt-editing environment) at zero cost. The up/down arrow buttons next to each stage row give touch users the same reorder capability through the same reorderStages() pure helper and reorderProjectStages server action. One feature, two input modalities, one code path. When the Gantt story needs true 2-D drag we can swap in dnd-kit then.

factory_decisions.decision: Two-phase reorder update: tombstone-with-negative-indices then write final slots
factory_decisions.context: reorderProjectStages writes a new order_index to every stage in a project. Today there is no UNIQUE(project_id, order_index) constraint, so a one-pass update would work. But partial densification (e.g., swapping s1 order 0↔1 with s2 order 1) could briefly assign two rows the same index during the sequence of updates.
alternatives_considered: Single-pass update with arbitrary order: works today, breaks silently if a UNIQUE constraint is ever added
Supabase RPC with a single transactional UPDATE ... FROM: requires a new migration + stored procedure for a 4-stage reorder
Delete-and-reinsert: loses stage ids, cascades tasks away
factory_decisions.rationale: Two passes — first push each stage to order_index = -(1 + position) as a tombstone, then write the final 0..N-1 index — guarantees no two rows ever share a positive order_index mid-update. No new infrastructure, cheap (2N updates for N stages, N≤~12 typical), and safe if a UNIQUE constraint shows up in a later migration. deleteStage uses the same densification pattern on remaining stages.

factory_decisions.decision: Delete confirmation driven by client-side task_count, server independently re-verifies
factory_decisions.context: AC-SM-3 vs AC-SM-4 gates on "stage has tasks." The page loads per-stage task counts; the client uses them to decide whether to show the dialog. But the server action also needs to decide whether to allow an unconfirmed delete.
alternatives_considered: Client-only decision, server always deletes when called: a stale/tampered client could delete a populated stage without confirmation
Server returns requiresConfirmation the first call, client retries with confirmed=true: doubles round trips for every delete
Skip the dialog entirely, always server-delete with cascade: loses AC-SM-3 warning requirement
factory_decisions.rationale: Client shows the dialog based on the task_count it loaded (fresh render = fresh count). Server action independently re-counts tasks — if taskCount > 0 and confirmed=false, it returns requiresConfirmation without deleting. The client ignores that fallback path in the happy case (it already showed the dialog) but the server guarantee means a stale count or a rogue client cannot delete populated stages without explicit confirmed=true. Defense in depth, one round trip in the common case.

factory_decisions.decision: Six-swatch color palette (hardcoded) instead of free-form color picker
factory_decisions.context: AC-SM-1 specifies a "color" field. Options: free-form <input type="color">, preset palette, hex text input.
alternatives_considered: Native <input type="color">: gives users infinite options that can clash with the brand palette; color wheel UI is inconsistent across browsers
Hex text input: requires validation copy + error UX for every typo
Larger preset (12+ swatches): decision paralysis, the Gantt only needs enough contrast to tell stages apart
factory_decisions.rationale: Six swatches drawn from the product's brand palette (earth brown, frame green, ochre, ink, slate blue, brick red) give every stage a distinct, on-brand color. Picker UI is a row of circular buttons with aria-pressed — accessible, instant, no typing. validateStageInput still accepts any valid hex so a future "custom color" story (or a direct DB edit) is not blocked. normalizeStageColor falls back to the earth-brown default if the stored value is ever corrupt.

factory_decisions.decision: Inclusive-day semantics for planned_start/planned_end (end = start + duration − 1)
factory_decisions.context: AC-TM-2 says "planned_end = planned_start + 2 days" for a 2-day task. Two interpretations: exclusive (start=06-01, end=06-03 → 2 nights) or inclusive (start=06-01, end=06-02 → first and last working day, 2 working days).
alternatives_considered: Exclusive: planned_end = planned_start + duration_days. Simpler arithmetic but a 1-day task would render as a zero-width Gantt bar and the end date is a day the task is NOT worked.
Inclusive: planned_end = planned_start + (duration_days − 1). Matches how humans read a schedule ("Monday–Tuesday = 2 days") and matches the existing seed data pattern.
factory_decisions.rationale: Checked the seeded O'Brien data + the template-apply cursor schedule — both treat planned_start and planned_end as the first and last working day of the task (inclusive). A 1-day task has planned_start = planned_end. The cursor schedule in lib/templates/apply.ts advances via `start = prev.planned_end + 1`, which is exactly AC-TM-4's "max(dep.planned_end) + 1 day" formula — consistent across the codebase.

factory_decisions.decision: AC-TM-3 "no deps AND no prior task" resolves to project.start_date for all no-deps cases
factory_decisions.context: AC-TM-3 specifies the no-deps-no-prior-task case but is silent on no-deps-WITH-prior-tasks-in-stage. template-apply uses a sequential cursor (each task follows the previous one in order_index). template-customization uses project.start_date as a placeholder.
alternatives_considered: Sequential cursor within the stage — infers an implicit dependency chain the user did not author. If the user really wants sequence, they can set depends_on explicitly.
Max end of prior tasks in stage — leaks "stage order" into date computation. Same problem: implicit chain.
project.start_date uniformly when depends_on is empty — predictable, self-documenting, matches the customization-story placeholder pattern.
factory_decisions.rationale: Dependencies are the single source of truth for sequencing. If a user adds a task with no dependencies, they get project.start_date and can fix it by either adding deps or editing the date directly in a later story. Explicit > implicit. Matches the template-customization decision to use start_date as the placeholder for newly-added tasks.

factory_decisions.decision: AC-TM-6 dependency cleanup relies on ON DELETE CASCADE on task_dependencies.depends_on_task_id
factory_decisions.context: The spec requires that deleting task A removes it from the depends_on array of every downstream task. Options: handle in application code, use the DB cascade, or both.
alternatives_considered: Application-layer: SELECT dependents, DELETE by task_id = A, then DELETE rows where depends_on_task_id = A. Three round trips, race conditions if another request is editing deps simultaneously.
DB cascade only: task_dependencies has ON DELETE CASCADE on BOTH foreign keys (task_id, depends_on_task_id). Deleting task A atomically removes every row that references A in either column.
Both layers for defense in depth: duplicates effort without improving guarantees.
factory_decisions.rationale: The schema already guarantees AC-TM-6 via CASCADE. Server action just deletes the task and RLS + CASCADE do the rest. Added a pure removeFromDependencies() helper for the UI to mirror the same behaviour in client state — so the task list re-renders correctly without a round trip.

factory_decisions.decision: updateTaskDuration holds planned_start fixed and recalculates only planned_end
factory_decisions.context: AC-TM-5 says "edits the duration_days field and saves, THEN the task's planned_end recalculates and cascades." Options: recompute planned_start from deps then extend end, or keep start stable and extend/shrink end.
alternatives_considered: Recompute planned_start from deps on every duration edit — could shift the start date if deps have changed since task creation, surprising the user when they just edited duration.
Hold start fixed, shift end only — matches the AC's "end recalculates" wording and is what cascade_task_dates() is designed for (it receives new_planned_end and propagates the delta downstream).
Re-run the full schedule — massive blast radius for a single edit.
factory_decisions.rationale: cascade_task_dates(p_task_id, p_new_planned_start, p_new_planned_end) expects the caller to pick the new end. Passing the current planned_start unchanged + new planned_end = start + (duration − 1) gives a clean delta the recursive CTE uses to shift every downstream task by the same offset. One RPC, one transaction, O(downstream) updates.

factory_decisions.decision: Add Task lives on /stages/:id, reached via an "Open" link on each stage row in /schedule
factory_decisions.context: AC-TM-1 says "clicks Add Task within [a stage]". Three possible homes: inline on /schedule (expand each stage), dedicated /stages/:id detail, or a full modal launched from the schedule list.
alternatives_considered: Inline-on-schedule: every stage on the schedule page expands into a task list. Crowds a Gantt-like screen; doesn't scale past ~3 tasks per stage.
/stages/:id detail page with full task CRUD: clean single-purpose screen, future-proof for per-stage editing, and the /stages/:id route already exists as a placeholder.
Global /tasks list: loses the stage scoping AC-TM-1 requires.
factory_decisions.rationale: The foundation already scaffolded /stages/[id]/page.tsx as a placeholder route and the /schedule StageManager already has per-stage rows. Adding an "Open" link to each row + rewriting the stage detail page as the task-CRUD screen uses existing infrastructure, keeps /schedule lightweight, and gives tasks a natural home for future attributes (materials, photos, trade assignment).

factory_decisions.decision: Trade delete uses explicit unassign-then-delete (no SET NULL FK)
factory_decisions.context: AC-TR-4 requires deleting a trade assigned to N tasks to null out task.trade_id for all N tasks. Options: rely on a SET NULL FK, run an UPDATE then DELETE in the server action, or wrap in an RPC/transaction.
alternatives_considered: Add ON DELETE SET NULL to tasks.trade_id FK — requires a new migration for a behaviour the UI already needs to orchestrate around a confirmation dialog; the schema is shared with other stories, migration churn is expensive.
Single DELETE with a pre-count and trust a future FK cascade — if the FK is ever added with RESTRICT (the default), the delete fails silently for assigned trades.
RPC wrapping both updates in a transaction — overkill for a two-statement sequence that can't meaningfully partially-fail at this scale.
factory_decisions.rationale: UPDATE tasks SET trade_id=null WHERE trade_id=X AND project_id=P, then DELETE FROM trades WHERE id=X. Two statements, both scoped by project_id so cross-project writes are impossible, no schema change, no RPC. Server recounts assignments independently before acting on confirmed=true, so a stale client cannot bypass the confirmation dialog.

factory_decisions.decision: Trade assignment on /tasks/:id is an inline optimistic <select>, not a modal
factory_decisions.context: AC-TR-2 says 'assigns it to a task via task edit form.' The existing Add Task form already has a trade dropdown; the detail screen needs a standalone control for re-assigning an existing task.
alternatives_considered: Open a full edit-task modal from the detail page — duplicates StageTasksManager's add form, and this story's scope is trade-contacts not task-edit.
Inline 'Edit' button that opens an edit drawer with every task field — scope creep into task-management follow-up work already flagged in factory_questions.
Dedicated /tasks/:id/edit route — extra navigation step for a single-field change.
factory_decisions.rationale: A single <select> element on the detail page changes trade_id in-place with an optimistic client update + server rollback on error. Matches the lightweight direct-manipulation pattern from StageManager's drag-reorder and avoids opening a form just to change one field. Other fields (name/notes/duration) are explicitly deferred to a future task-edit story per the existing factory_question.

factory_decisions.decision: formatTelHref strips all non-digit characters but preserves a single leading +
factory_decisions.context: AC-TR-3 requires 'a tap-to-call link using the tel: protocol.' Trade phone numbers are free-text — Irish mobile ('087 123 4567'), Irish landline with area code ('01 4567890'), international ('+353 1 456 7890'), US-style with parens ('(555) 123-4567'). tel: URIs accept digits and a leading + (RFC 3966).
alternatives_considered: Pass the raw string through to tel: — spaces and parens break dialer parsing on some Android builds and produce inconsistent behaviour across platforms.
Full RFC 3966 parser with country-code inference — overkill; we don't know the user's default country and most dialers DTRT with digits+leading-plus.
Refuse to build a link unless the input matches a strict regex — rejects valid numbers users type naturally, degrades AC-TR-3 to nothing.
factory_decisions.rationale: Strip to digits, preserve leading + if present. Returns null if no digits remain so the caller can render the phone as plain text instead of a broken link. Tested against Irish mobile/landline, international +353, US (555) formats. Keeps the raw phone string in the DB for display; only the href is normalized.

factory_decisions.decision: Assignment counts loaded server-side on /trades (no per-row query)
factory_decisions.context: The trade list needs per-trade assignment counts to render the delete warning with the right task count without a server round trip on every delete attempt. Options: N queries (one per trade), SQL GROUP BY via an RPC, or a single broad select.
alternatives_considered: One count() query per trade — N round trips for N trades; slow with any number of trades.
Custom rpc get_trade_assignment_counts() — new stored procedure for what one select can do.
Client-side assignment count derived from a tasks fetch — doubles the client payload for a page that doesn't otherwise need tasks.
factory_decisions.rationale: One SELECT trade_id FROM tasks WHERE project_id=P AND trade_id IS NOT NULL, then tally into a Map. One round trip, O(tasks) memory on the server, zero schema change. Projects won't realistically have thousands of tasks in this target domain (residential self-build).

factory_decisions.decision: order_by_date computed client+server from task.planned_start - lead_time_days, mirroring the DB trigger
factory_decisions.context: AC-ML-4 requires that editing lead_time_days recalculates order_by_date. The schema's cascade_task_dates trigger already computes this on task-date changes; the question was whether the material form should POST a pre-computed order_by_date or rely on the trigger.
alternatives_considered: Set order_by_date=NULL on insert/update and let a DB trigger derive it — requires a new BEFORE-INSERT/UPDATE trigger, more SQL infra for a one-line calculation
Run a follow-up UPDATE after insert that calls a stored proc — two round trips per save
Derive order_by_date at read time in the RSC — ignores the existing schema column and diverges from the cascade trigger
factory_decisions.rationale: computeOrderByDate() is a pure TypeScript function identical to the SQL the cascade trigger runs (planned_start minus lead_time_days). The server action computes it once on insert/update so the value stored matches what the trigger would overwrite on the next cascade. One round trip, single source of truth for the formula, easy to unit test with UTC date arithmetic.

factory_decisions.decision: Materials live inline on /tasks/:id as a TaskMaterialsManager section (not a dedicated /materials/:id route)
factory_decisions.context: AC-ML-1 says 'clicks Add Material on the task detail screen, THEN an inline form appears'. Affected screens also list /materials but no AC describes what belongs there. Choice: inline section, modal, or dedicated subroute.
alternatives_considered: Modal dialog like the trade add/edit flow — heavier UI for what the spec calls 'inline form'
Dedicated /tasks/:id/materials subroute — extra navigation step, breaks the 'everything about this task on one screen' mental model
Leave on the /materials list page — contradicts AC-ML-1's 'task detail screen' wording
factory_decisions.rationale: A section within /tasks/:id with an inline expandable form matches AC-ML-1 literally, keeps the task as the container for its materials (which is how the data model is scoped: materials.task_id NOT NULL), and reuses the existing TaskTradeAssigner layout on that page. The /materials index remains the future home for a cross-task materials dashboard (ordering, deliveries, inventory) — that belongs to a separate story.

factory_decisions.decision: lead_time_days validation collapses empty, negative, non-integer, and non-numeric inputs to a single error message
factory_decisions.context: AC-ML-3 specifies exactly one message ('Lead time must be 0 or more days') for 'empty or negative'. The input is a text field (so the user can also type 'abc' or '2.5'). Choice: multiple messages per failure mode, or one message that covers them all.
alternatives_considered: Separate messages for 'must be a number' / 'must be a whole number' / 'must be 0 or more' — more precise, but three different disabled-save states and more test surface
Silently coerce bad input (parseInt + floor) — changes the user's intent and hides typos
factory_decisions.rationale: The AC calls out exactly one copy string. Collapsing every invalid-input variant under that message keeps behaviour consistent with the AC verbatim, keeps the save-button-disabled logic simple (liveValidation.ok), and the HTML type=number input already filters most non-numeric keystrokes on keyboards that support it.

factory_decisions.decision: Save button disable is driven by a live validation on every render, not an onChange-computed error state
factory_decisions.context: AC-ML-3 says 'the save button is disabled' while lead_time is invalid. Two ways to express that: (a) recompute validity inside render, or (b) store an isValid flag and update it in each field's onChange.
alternatives_considered: onChange-synced isValid boolean + useEffect — adds a render + stale-state risk when form initializes on edit
Debounced validation — unnecessary for a synchronous pure function
Only validate on submit — would require leaving the save button enabled until click, contradicting AC-ML-3 literally
factory_decisions.rationale: validateMaterialInput is a pure O(1) function; calling it once per render is free and guarantees the disabled state always reflects the current form values, including when the form is first populated by Edit. formErrors is still only set on submit or server-error, so the user doesn't see red text until they try to save.

factory_decisions.decision: Include overdue not_ordered materials in /briefing Upcoming Orders (top of the sorted list)
factory_decisions.context: AC-OB-4 literal wording is "order_by_date within the next 7 days, sorted ascending". Taken strictly this excludes overdue items, but AC-OB-2 says overdue materials must render somewhere on /briefing or /materials, and hiding them from the briefing defeats the purpose of the section for a foreman planning their day.
alternatives_considered: Strictly [today, today+7] — hides overdue items from briefing entirely; foreman misses urgent orders
Separate "Overdue" subsection above Upcoming Orders — adds UI surface, duplicates the badge signal
Include order_by_date <= today+7 (chosen) — overdue items sort to the top by ISO date, one list, badge colors disambiguate
factory_decisions.rationale: selectUpcomingOrders filters to not_ordered + order_by_date <= today+7. Ascending ISO sort naturally places overdue dates first, and the red overdue badge disambiguates them from the amber due-soon items. One list, zero duplication, aligned with AC-OB-2 requirement that overdue materials appear in /briefing.

factory_decisions.decision: Badge boundaries: overdue = order_by_date < today; due_soon = 0 <= days <= 7 (inclusive both ends)
factory_decisions.context: AC-OB-2 says "in the past", AC-OB-3 says "within the next 7 days". "In the past" is unambiguous but "within the next 7 days" could mean [today, today+7] or [today+1, today+7]. Also: should the boundary at day 7 be inclusive?
alternatives_considered: Exclusive upper bound (< 7 days) — a material due exactly 7 days out would show no badge, then flip to due_soon tomorrow — jarring UX
Start window at today+1 — a material due today shows no badge despite being the most urgent pending item
factory_decisions.rationale: Inclusive on both ends matches "within the next 7 days" as a foreman reads it. day-diff >= 0 && <= 7 → due_soon, day-diff < 0 → overdue. Stable transitions day-to-day.

factory_decisions.decision: Deadline status is computed at render time from "today" + order_by_date, not persisted
factory_decisions.context: Badges change daily as today advances past each order_by_date. Options: (a) store a computed badge column + recompute via cron, (b) compute at render time from the existing order_by_date column.
alternatives_considered: Store badge state in a column + nightly cron — extra table columns, an extra cron job, drift between DB and reality between runs
Compute in a DB view — needs view maintenance, SQL time() calls vary by connection timezone
